summary: The PoC replicates the Formulas service Twig sandbox configuration and demonstrates that a crafted formula using range() and for tags causes unbounded memory allocation and CPU consumption. The PoC was not executed against a live Craft CMS instance because setting up the full Craft CMS + Commerce stack (MySQL, web server, framework configuration) in Docker is prohibitively complex for a self-contained reproduction bundle.
expected_security_impact: The exploit formula should cause the PHP process to allocate massive memory (>128 MB) and/or consume significant CPU time (>30 seconds), demonstrating that a single request can exhaust PHP worker resources and deny service to legitimate users.
actual_observed_result: The PoC was not executed. The reproduction bundle contains a standalone PHP script that replicates the exact Twig sandbox from Formulas.php and demonstrates the resource exhaustion pattern at the Twig template evaluation level.
verification_method: The verification script measures peak memory usage and execution time of a crafted DoS formula versus a benign formula. A successful demonstration is defined as the DoS formula consuming >10x the memory or >10x the time of a benign formula.
limitations_or_blockers: The PoC was not executed against a live Craft CMS instance because Craft Commerce requires the full Craft CMS framework, a MySQL/PostgreSQL database, web server configuration, and application setup that cannot be reliably automated in a self-contained Docker reproduction bundle.
The standalone PoC exercises the same Twig sandbox code path (createTemplate + render with the identical SecurityPolicy) but bypasses the HTTP controller layer.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo for reference.
